_meta.author: national-autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 sitting Houston/Harris TX House Democrats HD144-148 (mary-ann-perez, christina-morales, lauren-ashley-simmons, jolanda-jones, penny-morales-shaw). State tier. Cited, primary-source where available (TX House Journal roll calls for Shaw). All five are consistent Houston-area progressives — NO cross-party deviations found. TAL/TRTL 42-55% scores are artifacts of pro-life orgs crediting bipartisan maternal-health bills, NOT pro-life votes; not scored as credit. Shaw was ABSENT/EXCUSED on the SB2 final vote (cell left null, not scored). Freedom Index erroneously reports several of these members voting FOR HB1927/abortion bills — refuted by primary roll calls; disregarded.

mary-ann-perez@TX.notes_set: TX House HD-144 (Pasadena/SE Houston), Harris County. In office since 2013 (with a 2015-2017 gap). Won the contested March 3, 2026 Democratic primary (3-way), advancing to the Nov 3, 2026 general vs. David Flores (R). Voted AGAINST SB2 vouchers (2025, near-party-line marquee, every present Democrat opposed); AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban — not among the 4 Democratic crossovers); AGAINST HB1927 permitless carry (2021 primary roll call — NOTE the John Birch Freedom Index erroneously lists her HB1927 vote as 'Yes'; the primary roll call shows NO, disregard the index); AGAINST SB10 Ten Commandments (2025). Participated in the 2021 DC quorum break to block election bill SB1. Texas Alliance for Life 2025: 55% (aggregate artifact of bipartisan maternal-health bills, NOT a pro-life vote — not credited). No pro-choice PAC endorsement located, so sanctity cells left null rather than scored FALSE. Confidence evidence_state.

0.note: No statement affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: No pro-life record; TAL 55% is aggregate (bipartisan maternal-health bills), not an abortion-restriction vote. No pro-choice endorsement located either — left null rather than inferred.

4.note: No evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding.

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB14 (2023 ban on transgender medical interventions for minors); not among the 4 Democratic crossovers (Thierry, Dutton, Herrero, King) — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Voted against SB12/SB13 (2025 parents'-rights/library bills) but no LGBTQ-promotion endorsement confirmed; left null.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB2 school-choice/ESA vouchers (2025, 86-61 near-party-line; every present Democrat opposed).

3.note: Voted NO on SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms, 2025) — contrary to public-square Christian symbols.
Participated in the 2021 DC quorum break to block election bill SB1 (which restricted mail-in/drop-box voting) — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting.

2.note: No name-confirmed SB4 roll call located; not scored.

0.note: Voted AGAINST HB1927 constitutional/permitless carry (2021) per the primary roll call (the 7 Democratic 'yes' votes did not include her). Freedom Index erroneously reports 'Yes' — disregarded.

christina-morales@TX.notes_set: TX House HD-145 (East End/Houston), Harris County. In office since 2019 (succeeded Carol Alvarado). Ran UNOPPOSED in the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary; advances to Nov 3, 2026 general vs. Inocencia Moreno (R). Now chairs the Texas House Democratic Campaign Committee (THDCC) for 2026. Annie's List endorsed champion (pro-choice women's PAC). Voted AGAINST SB2 vouchers (2025 near-party-line); AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban — not among the 4 Democratic crossovers). Participated in the 2021 DC quorum break (named plaintiff in the Democrats' lawsuit) to block election bill SB1; also joined the Aug 2025 redistricting quorum break. Texas Alliance for Life 2025: 44% (aggregate artifact of bipartisan health bills, contradicted by her Annie's List endorsement — NOT credited as pro-life). Confidence evidence_state.

0.note: No statement affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Annie's List endorsed champion (pro-choice women's PAC) — does not advocate abortion abolition. TAL 44% is an aggregate health-bill artifact, not a pro-life vote.

4.note: Annie's List is a candidate PAC, not the abortion industry per the cell's named groups (PP/NARAL/EMILY's) — not scored.

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban); not among the 4 Democratic crossovers — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Voted against book/library and parents'-rights bills but no LGBTQ-promotion endorsement confirmed; left null.

0.note: In the Democratic 'no' bloc on SB2 school-choice/ESA vouchers (2025 near-party-line, every present Democrat opposed).

3.note: No individual SB10 (Ten Commandments) roll call located.
Named plaintiff / participant in the 2021 DC quorum break to block election bill SB1 (restricting mail-in/drop boxes) — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting.

2.note: No name-confirmed SB4 roll call located; not scored.

0.note: No name-confirmed HB1927 roll call located (Democratic crossover list does not include her, but not individually verified); not scored.

lauren-ashley-simmons@TX.notes_set: TX House HD-146 (Houston), Harris County. Freshman — defeated incumbent Shawn Thierry in the 2024 Democratic runoff, took office Jan 2025; ONLY the 89th (2025) session, so 2021/2023 votes are N/A. Ran UNOPPOSED in the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary; advances to Nov 3, 2026 general. House Democratic Caucus Freshman of the Year; union organizer background (CWA shop steward). Self-identifies as queer. Pro-choice (direct quote: 'Abortion is a health care procedure'); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes, Avow Texas, Annie's List, LPAC. Endorsed by Equality Texas c4, HRC, LGBTQ Victory Fund, Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus; ran explicitly against Thierry's trans-care/sports votes. In the Democratic 'no' bloc on SB2 vouchers (2025). TRTL/TAL 2025: 42% (aggregate health-bill artifact, NOT a pro-life vote — not credited). SB10 was a 'mostly' party-line vote, not unanimous, and her individual row was not confirmed — left null. Confidence evidence_state.

0.note: No statement affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice (own quote: 'Abortion is a health care procedure... without government interference'; vowed to repeal the Texas abortion ban); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes, Avow, Annie's List — does not advocate abolition.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Texas Votes ENDORSED her, but no abortion-industry PAC funding/contribution confirmed (endorsement is not funding) — left null.

0.note: No direct same-sex-marriage statement located (she is pro-LGBTQ generally).

2.note: Campaigned explicitly against predecessor Thierry's votes restricting gender-affirming care for minors and trans sports participation; opposes those restrictions — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Endorsed by Equality Texas c4, HRC, LGBTQ Victory Fund, Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus; campaign explicitly champions LGBTQIA+ policy — contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: In the Democratic 'no' bloc on SB2 school-choice/ESA vouchers (2025, every present Democrat opposed); campaigns to keep public schools strong.

3.note: SB10 (Ten Commandments) was a 'mostly' party-line vote, not unanimous, and her individual row was not confirmed — left null rather than inferred.

1.note: No specific voter-ID vote isolated; slogan-level opposition to 'voting restrictions' only.

3.note: Not in office 2021; no specific vote located.

2.note: No evidence (not in office for SB4 2023).

jolanda-jones@TX.notes_set: TX House HD-147 (Houston), Harris County. In office since 2022 (special election to succeed Garnet Coleman); sessions 2023 + 2025 only (not 2021). Won the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary UNOPPOSED; faces Theodis Daniel (R) in November. Also running for U.S. Congress (TX-18 special) but remains the sitting HD-147 rep. Criminal-defense attorney; first out Black lesbian in the TX Legislature and a vocal marriage-equality champion (endorsed by Equality PAC, LPAC, LGBTQ+ Victory Fund). Voted AGAINST SB2 vouchers (2025, 'welfare for the wealthy'); AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban — not among the 4 Democratic crossovers); opposed SB10 Ten Commandments as unconstitutional; called SB4 (2023) 'racist' and opposed it. Justice-reform record: sponsored HB765 (police liability insurance), HB1765 (no-knock limits), HB867 (prosecutor dismissal discretion), HB463 (drug expunction). YCT 10/100, Freedom Index 9% (aggregate). Confidence evidence_state.

0.note: No statement affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Conservative scorecards rate her near-zero, but no specific pro-choice statement or abortion-PAC endorsement was cited — left null rather than inferred from aggregate ratings.

4.note: No evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: First out Black lesbian in the TX Legislature and a documented marriage-equality champion — does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: Champion of marriage equality, endorsed by LGBTQ+ Victory Fund / LPAC / Equality PAC — supports, does not oppose, same-sex marriage.

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban), vowing to 'protect us all'; not among the 4 Democratic crossovers — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Vocal LGBTQ champion endorsed by national LGBTQ PACs — contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB2 school-choice/ESA vouchers (2025), calling it 'welfare for the wealthy.'

3.note: Publicly opposed SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms, 2025) as unconstitutional / a threat to non-Christian religious freedom — contrary to supporting public-square Christian symbols.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC; YCT/Freedom Index are aggregate.

3.note: Opposes GOP voting restrictions generally but not in office for the 2021 quorum break; no specific vote located — left null.

0.note: Police-accountability record (helped shut HPD crime lab; HB765 police liability insurance) but no clean 'defund' vote — left null at the high bar.

1.note: Criminal-defense attorney with a justice-reform record: sponsored HB867 (prosecutor dismissal discretion), HB1765 (no-knock limits), HB463 (drug expunction), favoring pretrial intervention — contrary to opposing soft-on-crime/prosecutorial-leniency policy.
Opposes Operation Lone Star framing but no clean deportation vote isolated.

2.note: Opposed SB4 (2023 state illegal-entry crime / anti-sanctuary enforcement), calling it 'racist' — contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

0.note: Not in office for HB1927 (2021); conservative 2A scorecards rate her 0 but no specific vote/statement located — left null.

penny-morales-shaw@TX.notes_set: TX House HD-148 (Houston), Harris County. In office since Jan 2021; sessions 87th/88th/89th. Running in the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary (incumbent; no Democratic challenger surfaced — treat 'unopposed' as unconfirmed); GOP candidate Amanda LaBrie. Now on the House Elections Committee. Primary-source TX House Journal roll calls: voted AGAINST HB1927 permitless carry (2021, recorded in the Nays — NOTE: do not confuse with Eddie Morales (D) who voted YES); AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban, in the Nays); on SB10 Ten Commandments (2025) recorded Absent but filed a Statement of Vote 'I would have voted no.' ABSENT/EXCUSED on the SB2 voucher final passage (no position recorded — cell left null). Participated in the 2021 DC quorum break to block SB1. Annie's List endorsed; Texas Women's Health Caucus + House LGBTQ Caucus member. ILA Liberty Index 17%, TFR grade F (aggregate). Freedom Index auto-summary erroneously reports FOR-votes on guns/abortion — refuted by the primary House Journal; disregarded. Confidence evidence_state.

0.note: No statement affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Annie's List endorsed; member of the Texas Women's Health Caucus — pro-choice, does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Annie's List is a candidate PAC, not the abortion industry per the cell's named groups — not scored.

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB14 (2023 trans-minor-care ban) per the House Journal (Record 1688, in the Nays) — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

4.note: Member of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus; campaign site advocates LGBTQIA+ legal protections — contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: ABSENT/EXCUSED on the SB2 voucher final passage (Record 300, 4/17/2025); no position recorded — left null (her only SB2 record vote was a procedural postponement, not a stance).

3.note: On SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms, 2025) filed a Statement of Vote: 'I would have voted no' — contrary to supporting public-square Christian symbols.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC; ILA/TFR are aggregate.

3.note: Participated in the 2021 DC quorum break to block election bill SB1 (restricting mail-in/drop boxes) — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting.

0.note: No 'defund' statement found; left null at the high bar.

1.note: Campaign site favors 'rehabilitative justice... judicial discretion and pretrial intervention' to reduce jail population — contrary to opposing soft-on-crime/leniency policy.

2.note: SB4 individual vote not located in the 4th-special-session journal; not scored.

0.note: Voted AGAINST HB1927 constitutional/permitless carry (2021) per the House Journal (Record 298, in the Nays). NOTE: 'Morales, E.' (Eddie Morales) is in the Yeas — Penny Morales Shaw is in the Nays.
